title: Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES CHILDREN'S INTENSIVE IN-HOME SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides flexible, family-centered, culturally sensitive case management services that help families care for their children with significant disabilities in their homes. The program provides services based on the needs of the child and family while using state and federal resources efficiently. CIIS has three specialized sections to address the needs of children with disabilities. A child could qualify and receive services through the Intensive Behavior, Medically Involved or Medically Fragile Programs. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Varies by program: Most programs ages 17 and younger may receive services up to age 21 if program requirements are met Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 945-5811 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CHILDREN'S INTENSIVE IN-HOME SERVICES
